Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me compile my findings. ## Thorough T3 Code Monorepo Analysis I've completed a comprehensive exploration of the T3 Code monorepo. Here's the detailed picture: --- ## 1. **Overall Directory Structure** ``` ├── apps/ │ ├── web/ (React 19 frontend - Vite + TanStack Router) │ ├── server/ (Backend server) │ ├── desktop/ (Electron app) │ └── marketing/ (Marketing site) ├── packages/ │ ├── shared/ (TypeScript utilities using Effect) │ └── contracts/ (Type contracts & schemas using Effect) ├── tsconfig.base.json (Base TypeScript config) ├── turbo.json (Monorepo orchestration) └── package.json (Root workspace config) ``` --- ## 2. **packages/shared Structure** **Location:** `/packages/shared` **Purpose:** Shared utilities library (non-UI, backend-focused) **TypeScript Pattern:** Uses Effect library (functional programming) **Export pattern (package.json exports):** ```json { "exports": { "./model": "./src/model.ts", "./git": "./src/git.ts", "./logging": "./src/logging.ts", "./shell": "./src/shell.ts", "./Net": "./src/Net.ts" } } ``` **Key files in `src/`:** - `model.ts` - Model/provider logic - `git.ts` - Git utilities - `logging.ts` - Logging setup - `shell.ts` - Shell utilities - `Net.ts` - Network utilities - Test files (`.test.ts`) **Note:** This package is for backend/shared logic, NOT UI. It uses Effect schema validation patterns. --- ## 3. **apps/web Structure** **Technology Stack:** - **Framework:** React 19 with React 19 compiler - **Router:** TanStack React Router (file-based routing in `src/routes/`) - **Styling:** Tailwind CSS 4 with CSS variables (not CSS modules) - **UI Components:** Base UI React + custom UI library - **State Management:** Zustand (for app state) - **Build Tool:** Vite with tailwindcss plugin **Key Directory Structure:** ``` src/ ├── main.tsx (Entry point) ├── index.html (Root HTML) ├── index.css (Global styles - Tailwind + CSS variables) ├── router.ts (Router setup) ├── store.ts (Zustand main store) ├── appSettings.ts (Settings state - useSyncExternalStore pattern) ├── hooks/ │ ├── useTheme.ts (EXISTING THEME HOOK - uses useSyncExternalStore!) │ ├── useMediaQuery.ts │ └── useTurnDiffSummaries.ts ├── components/ │ ├── ui/ (Base UI components - 40 files) │ ├── Sidebar.tsx │ ├── ChatView.tsx │ └── ... (major components) ├── lib/ │ ├── utils.ts (cn() utility for className merging) │ ├── serverReactQuery.ts │ ├── gitReactQuery.ts │ └── ... (query helpers) ├── routes/ │ ├── __root.tsx (Root layout with ToastProvider/AnchoredToastProvider) │ ├── _chat.tsx │ ├── _chat.$threadId.tsx │ └── _chat.settings.tsx └── ... (logic files) ``` --- ## 4. **Bootstrap Process** **index.html:** ```html
``` **main.tsx:** ```typescript import { RouterProvider } from "@tanstack/react-router"; import { createHashHistory, createBrowserHistory } from "@tanstack/react-router"; const history = isElectron ? createHashHistory() : createBrowserHistory(); const router = getRouter(history); ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById("root")).render( ); ``` **Root Route (__root.tsx):** - Uses `createRootRouteWithContext<{ queryClient: QueryClient }>()` - Wraps content with **ToastProvider** and **AnchoredToastProvider** - Has error boundary (`RootRouteErrorView`) - Renders `` for child routes --- ## 5. **Existing Theming & CSS Variables** **Current Theme System (hooks/useTheme.ts):** - **Pattern:** `useSyncExternalStore` for external state management - **Storage:** localStorage with key `"t3code:theme"` - **Modes:** "light" | "dark" | "system" - **Key Functions:** - `useTheme()` - returns `{ theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme }` - `getStored()` - reads from localStorage - `applyTheme(theme, suppressTransitions)` - applies to DOM (`.dark` class toggle) - Listens to system preference changes via `matchMedia("(prefers-color-scheme: dark)")` - Prevents flash on page load via immediate `applyTheme(getStored())` **CSS Variable System (index.css):** - Uses **Tailwind CSS 4** with `@import "tailwindcss"` - **Custom variant:** `@custom-variant dark (&:is(.dark, .dark *))` - **Theme inline definition:** Variables mapped to Tailwind theme: ```css --color-primary, --color-primary-foreground --color-secondary, --color-secondary-foreground --color-background, --foreground --color-card, --color-card-foreground --color-border, --color-input, --color-ring --color-accent, --color-muted, --color-destructive --color-info, --color-success, --color-warning --radius, --radius-sm, --radius-md, etc. ``` - **Light mode:** oklch colors + neutral palette - **Dark mode:** Adjusted oklch brightness + darker neutrals - **Special behavior:** `.no-transitions` class suppresses animations during theme change - **Base styles:** Applied via `@layer base` - borders, body background, etc. --- ## 6. **TypeScript Patterns** **Effect-based schemas (from packages/contracts):** ```typescript // Effect Schema pattern (validation + type derivation) const AppSettingsSchema = Schema.Struct({ codexBinaryPath: Schema.String.check(Schema.isMaxLength(4096)), confirmThreadDelete: Schema.Boolean, // ... }); export type AppSettings = typeof AppSettingsSchema.Type; ``` **Type vs Interface:** - **Contracts use `type`** - Most type definitions use `type` for unions and schemas - **UI components use `interface`** - React component props use `interface` (see sidebar.tsx) - **Example mixing:** ```typescript // From sidebar.tsx - interface for context type SidebarContextProps = { ... }; const SidebarContext = React.createContext(null); ``` **Pattern for IDs:** Branded types via Effect ```typescript export type ThreadId = typeof ThreadId.Type; // Branded primitive type ``` --- ## 7. **packages/shared/src Directory Details** All files are re-exported via package.json exports: - `model.ts` - ~50 lines, model resolution functions - `git.ts` - Git command utilities - `logging.ts` - Logging infrastructure - `shell.ts` - Shell execution - `Net.ts` - HTTP/Network utilities - Test files for each module **No UI components in shared** - it's purely backend/utility focused. --- ## 8. **React Context Providers in apps/web** **Confirmed providers:** 1. **RouterProvider** (TanStack Router) - at root 2. **ToastProvider** - in `__root.tsx` (from `components/ui/toast.tsx`) 3. **AnchoredToastProvider** - in `__root.tsx` (handles anchored positioning) 4. **QueryClientProvider** - implied via QueryClient context (for React Query) **Context patterns used:** - **Sidebar:** `SidebarContext` + `SidebarInstanceContext` (React.createContext) - **Custom contexts follow pattern:** Create context with null default, throw if used outside provider, export hook --- ## 9. **Storage & State Management Patterns** **useSyncExternalStore pattern** (used for theme and app settings): ```typescript function subscribe(listener: () => void): () => void { listeners.push(listener); window.addEventListener("storage", handleStorage); // Multi-tab sync return () => { /* cleanup */ }; } function getSnapshot(): State { /* return current state */ } export function useHook() { const state = useSyncExternalStore(subscribe, getSnapshot); const setState = useCallback((next) => { localStorage.setItem(KEY, next); emitChange(); // Notify all subscribers }, []); return { state, setState }; } ``` **Zustand pattern** (for complex app state): ```typescript // store.ts uses create() from zustand // Multi-tab sync via localStorage // Persisted state versioning (v8 current) ``` --- ## 10. **UI Component Library** **Base UI React** + **Class Variance Authority (CVA)** **Components (40+ UI components):** - button, input, select, combobox, menu, popover - dialog, sheet, alert-dialog - checkbox, radio-group, toggle, toggle-group - scroll-area, separator, skeleton - badge, card, group - Custom components using CVA for variants **Pattern example (button.tsx):** ```typescript const buttonVariants = cva( "base-classes...", { variants: { variant: { default: "...", outline: "...", destructive: "...", }, size: { sm, default, lg, ... } } } ); export function Button({ variant = "default", size = "default", ...props }: ButtonProps) { return

content.text:
Everything looks correct. Here's a summary of all the changes made:

---

## What was built

### 1. `packages/shared/src/themes.ts` *(new)*
Defines the theme contract with no runtime dependencies — pure TypeScript:
- **`ThemeColors`** interface: `background`, `foreground`, `accent`, `sidebar`, `editor`, `terminal`, `statusBar`
- **`Theme`** interface: id, name, isDark flag, colors, fontFamily, fontSize
- **3 built-in themes**: `t3-dark` (deep black + indigo), `t3-light` (white + indigo), `t3-midnight` (GitHub-dark navy + green)
- **`getTheme(id)`** and **`listThemes()`** exported functions

### 2. `packages/shared/package.json` *(modified)*
Added the `"./themes"` subpath export so web (and any other consumer) can `import ... from "@t3tools/shared/themes"`.

### 3. `apps/web/src/hooks/useCustomTheme.tsx` *(new)*
Follows the exact same `useSyncExternalStore` pattern as the existing `useTheme.ts`:
- Persists the selected theme ID to `localStorage` (`t3code:custom-theme`, defaulting to `t3-dark`)
- `applyCustomTheme()` sets **inline CSS custom properties** on `<html>` — inline style wins over all stylesheet rules, including the dark-mode `@variant dark` block, so the theme is always authoritative
- Also toggles the `.dark` class and `color-scheme` so Tailwind `dark:` variants and browser system UI (scrollbars, inputs) match
- Exports **`CustomThemeProvider`** (React context) and **`useCustomTheme()`** hook returning `{ theme, setTheme, availableThemes }`
- Multi-tab sync via `StorageEvent` listener

### 4. `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` *(new)*
A radio-group of theme cards styled to match the existing settings page pattern:
- Three colour swatches per card (background · accent · statusBar) rendered as real colour circles
- Active badge matches the Appearance section style
- Dark/Light label + font size in the subtitle

### 5. `apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx` *(modified)*
Wraps the connected app tree with `<CustomThemeProvider>` so any component can call `useCustomTheme()`.

### 6. `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` *(modified)*
Adds a **"Color Theme"** section directly below the existing Appearance section, explaining that the selected theme overrides the light/dark preference, then renders `<ThemeSelector />`.
